summary: Iteration 5 testing complete. Backend: 76/76 pytest passing (13 new iter5 + 63 regression iter1+2+3+4). Frontend: Reports page email-doctor-card sends to unverified address and shows full Resend test-mode toast (not generic 'Email send failed'). Dashboard WeeklyInsight 'Email me this' button (weekly-insight-email) appears once insight text exists and triggers digest; on tester@anchor.app account the digest correctly returns the friendly 400 'test mode — your account email is not verified' toast. Milestones page renders all 9 stat cards (ms-streak, ms-best-protein, ms-protein-streak, ms-best-rating, ms-best-week, ms-med-adherence, ms-goals-completed, ms-protein-compliance, ms-diary-count) and community-not-ready banner is visible (1 of 5 users). Cross-user isolation verified for /api/milestones via fresh second user. /api/community/averages privacy threshold of 5 users enforced (nulls returned, users_count exposed). Resend test-mode error correctly converted to 400 with 'test mode' + 'verify a domain' in detail (not 502).

issues: Pre-existing recharts ResponsiveContainer width(-1)/height(-1) warning persists from iter4 (cosmetic only).

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1345 lines (was 1090 in iter4). Strongly recommend splitting into routers: email_router, milestones_router, community_router, share_router, reports_router. Past the 700-line guideline by ~2x.

Duplicate `app.include_router(api)` at lines 1090 and 1345 — the second call is a no-op (FastAPI re-registers same routes), but it's confusing and should be removed. The first one means /api/reports/email, /api/insights/email-digest, /api/milestones, /api/community/averages are technically registered AFTER include_router but the api router object is mutable, so it still works. Fragile pattern — move include_router to the very end.
CORSMiddleware is added AFTER routes are mounted (line 1348). FastAPI handles this for our app, but conventional order is middleware before include_router; consider reordering.

/api/reports/email and /api/insights/email-digest both manually parse Exception messages for Resend's 'only send testing emails' string. Acceptable, but the magic string is duplicated in two places — extract to a helper or check resend.exceptions.* type when available.

/api/milestones loads ALL meals + diary + med_logs from history (to_list(50000)) — fine for early users but will not scale. For users with years of data, consider date-bounding the streak/compliance calculation to last 365 days, or pre-aggregating.

/api/community/averages mood aggregation does NOT enforce the same min-users threshold separately — avg_mood is set BEFORE the privacy guard, then overwritten to null only when unique_users (from meals) < 5. If a user logs diary but not meals, mood unique-users count is never checked. Low-risk for current product but worth a comment / unified guard.

/api/community/averages defines `month_ago = today - 29 days` then matches `date: {$gte: month_ago}` against meals.date (string ISO). Works because ISO date strings compare lexicographically — but a comment explaining this would help future devs.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/conftest.py (load_dotenv from /app/backend/.env so tests can run from /app or /app/backend without env leakage)

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all three new flows (email report, email digest, milestones) verified live with toasts captured

Real Resend email was sent to daveseletto@gmail.com during happy-path test (subject 'Riley's recovery report — Last 7 days').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 5 complete. 76 backend tests in /app/backend/tests/ (run from either /app or /app/backend — conftest loads .env). Reports/email + insights/email-digest both correctly translate Resend test-mode errors to 400. /api/milestones returns the documented 13-key shape and is user-scoped. /api/community/averages enforces 5-user privacy threshold. server.py is now ~1345 lines and should be modularized next iteration. Note duplicated app.include_router(api) call in server.py.
